AY'S DECLARATION OPINING THAT JOINT COMPOUNDS CONTAINED ASBESTOS THROUGH THE MID-TO-LATE 1970S, CONTAINED IN PARAGRAPHS 109, 111, 112, 116, 118, 119, 129-132. DEFENDANT'S OTHER OBJECTIONS TO MR. RULING:
DEFENDANT DYNALECTRIC COMPANY'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT

Hearing held and tentative ruling adopted: Defendant Dynalectric Company's motion for summary judgment is denied. Defendant failed to sustain its burden of demonstrating that plaintiff does not possess and cannot reasonably obtain evidence that he was exposed to asbestos-containing products or materials attributable to defendant. Specifically, defendant failed to demonstrate that plaintiff has no knowledge whether Lera Electric employees exposed him to asbestos-containing joint compound, an exposure identified in plaintiff's responses to special interrogatories but not addressed in defendant's separate statement. This failure precludes defendant from successfully invoking the factually devoid prong of Aguilar v. Atlantic Richfield Co. (2001) 25 Cal.4th 826. Order signed in open court. James P. Nevin of BRAYTON & PURCELL LLP (415-898-1555), appearing for plaintiff / opposing party Thomas Duffy

RULING:
DEFENDANT D. ZELINSKY & SONS, INC.'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT

Hearing held and tentative ruling adopted: Defendant D. Zelinsky & Sons, Inc.'s motion for summary judgment is granted. Defendant sustained its initial burden of demonstrating that plaintiff does not possess and cannot reasonably obtain evidence that he was exposed to asbestos-containing products or materials attributable to defendant. Plaintiff failed to present admissible evidence to create a triable issue whether he was exposed to asbestos-containing products or materials attributable to defendant. Specifically, plaintiff failed to present admissible evidence to create a triable issue whether the drywall mud to which defendant's employees exposed him contained asbestos. Mr. Ay's declaration demonstrates that not all joint compounds during the time contained asbestos. Defendant's objections to plaintiff's declaration are overruled. Defendant's objections to Charles Ay's declaration are sustained as to paragraphs 134-135, as well as to all the statements throughout Mr. Ay's declaration opining that joint compounds contained asbestos through the mid-to-late 1970s, contained in paragraphs 109, 111, 112, 116, 118, 119, 129-132. Defendant's other objections to Mr. Ay's declaration are overruled. Prevailing party to prepare a proposed order in compliance with CRC 3.1312. Judge: Cynthia Ming-mei Lee, Reporter: Beth E. Grant of AIKEN WELCH, CSR #10943 (Tel: 510-451-1580) (503/CML)
